name: Mem.ai API
what_it_does: Mem is an AI-first note-taking and knowledge management tool with an API that allows agents to read and write to a user's personal knowledge base. Automatically organizes notes with AI, surfaces relevant context, and provides semantic search over personal knowledge.
use_cases: Agents reading from a user's personal knowledge base during task execution
Writing notes and memories from agent conversations to persistent storage
Semantic search over a user's personal notes and documents
Building personal AI assistants with access to user's historical context
Knowledge management agents that curate and connect information
not_for: Team or enterprise knowledge management (limited collaboration features)
Structured database use cases — this is unstructured notes
High-volume programmatic writing (rate limits designed for personal use)
Mission-critical data storage (not a reliable database replacement)
best_when: You're building a personal AI assistant that needs access to the user's knowledge base and notes, and the user is already a Mem user.
avoid_when: You need team collaboration, structured data, or a reliable programmatic knowledge base for production agents.

auth.notes: API key per user account. No scoped access — full account read/write. API access requires paid Mem plan.

pricing.paid_tiers: Mem X: $14.99/month or $8.33/month annually. API access included in paid plans.
pricing.notes: API access requires paid subscription. Reasonable for personal use cases.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; personal-use rate limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API is not the primary product focus — documentation is sparse and error messages are not well-structured for programmatic handling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency support. Repeated writes create duplicate notes.
known_agent_gotchas: API documentation is sparse — behavior must be inferred from experimentation
Rate limits not publicly documented — production use is risky without testing
No webhook support — agents must poll for new notes
No granular scopes — API key has full read/write to user's knowledge base
MCP server is community-maintained, not official
No idempotency — duplicate note creation on retry
agent_readiness.security_notes: Personal data product with basic API key security. No scoped permissions — API key has full access. Not suitable for enterprise data handling.
